dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI won the day by avoiding the more one-sided criticism aimed elsewhere: HN reacted skeptically to a ChatGPT cognitive-debt paper, but OpenAI also picked up concrete GPT-5 praise in benchmark chatter around Anthropic's take-home assignment, leaving it with the best net footing among tracked providers [E1][E2].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI finished first because its direct negative signal was partly offset by a meaningful positive comparison: the ChatGPT study sparked concern about overreliance and weaker recall, yet GPT-5 was also singled out as performing well on Anthropic's challenge thread [E1][E2]. That combination beat providers whose coverage was more consistently critical or absent.

openai.judgementSnippet: The day centered on a critical 'Your brain on ChatGPT' discussion about cognitive debt and weaker internal understanding, but GPT-5 also got a real lift from users reporting strong results on Anthropic's take-home benchmark [E1][E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude's new constitution drew the strongest reaction, with commenters questioning subjective ethics, PR framing, and possible exceptions for special-use models; praise for Anthropic's open-sourced take-home and recruiting style, plus news of Claude Code rendering fixes, softened but did not erase that drag [E3][E4][E5].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini's clearest signal came from the Anthropic take-home thread, where one commenter said Gemini CLI ran endlessly on the task and another benchmark table showed Gemini 3 Pro hitting the worst-cycle ceiling [E6].

evidence.summary: The front-page story 'Your brain on ChatGPT' directly targeted ChatGPT, and top comments echoed the concern by comparing habitual AI use to GPS dependence and saying vibe-coded work can weaken a user's internal model of the system.

In Anthropic's take-home assignment thread, a detailed user benchmark listed several GPT-5 variants and said gpt-5-2 did slightly better than Claude Opus in much less time, giving OpenAI a meaningful positive comparison signal.

The 'Claude's new constitution' thread was dominated by skepticism: commenters criticized the document as relativistic, possibly just PR or system-prompt branding, and worried that special-use models might not follow the same constraints.

Anthropic's open-sourced take-home assignment was received as an interesting, fun optimization problem, and one commenter explicitly said it was a much better recruiting approach than standard LeetCode-style interviews.

The Claude Chill story surfaced a long-standing Claude Code flicker annoyance, but an Anthropic employee said a rewritten differential renderer had just shipped broadly and upstream terminal support was improving.

Google Gemini was discussed in the Anthropic take-home thread when a commenter joked Gemini CLI had been running nonstop for 20 minutes, and another comparison table showed Gemini 3 Pro preview stuck at the worst-cycle ceiling.
